message:
test(ft-conformance): add exp/log/log2/log10 libm subprocess conformance

Locks the f64 base exp/log surface (exp / ln / log2 / log10) vs
platform libm. Companion to the existing trig / hyperbolic / expm1+
log1p libm harnesses: same pattern, but covers the four base
exp/log primitives that the others build on top of.

Both sides hit glibc through equivalent FFI paths (Rust f64::exp /
ln / log2 / log10 via libstd's libc shim; Python via ctypes-dlopened
libm), so the test requires *bit-exact* agreement (no ULP fudge
factor) across the entire f64 domain, NaN-payload-aware. Any future
replacement of the Rust f64 method with a hand-rolled approximation
— the same class of regression we hit on erf and lgamma — would
surface here immediately.

Inputs split by op family because the cliffs differ:

  * exp: 47 inputs covering trivial signs, the small-x Taylor regime
    (1e-3 .. 5e-324 ± symmetric), smooth interior (0.5 .. 50 ±),
    the +710 overflow cliff sweep (500 / 700 / 709 / 709.78 / 709.79
    / 710 / 711) where exp transitions to +inf, the symmetric -745
    underflow cliff sweep where exp drops to 0 / subnormals, plus
    transcendental constants and inf/nan.

  * log family (shared inputs for ln/log2/log10): 38 inputs covering
    domain edges (0.0 / -0.0 / MIN_POSITIVE / 5e-324 → -inf), the
    integer-output specials (1 → 0; 2 → log_b(2); 4/8/10/100/1000),
    small-positive negative-output magnitudes (0.001 .. 1e-300),
    large-positive (1e10 .. 1e300 .. f64::MAX), out-of-domain
    negative inputs (must yield NaN bit-identically), and inf/nan.

Total ~161 comparisons (47 exp + 38×3 log/log2/log10), well above
the 50-input contract bar. Both scalar and tensor batch paths
exercised.

The expm1+log1p harness already covered the *small-argument*
numerically-stable variants; this one covers the bare exp/log
family with its *large-argument* overflow + domain-error edges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
